title: The role of management in facilitating communication
summary: 9609 A Level — how managers enable effective communication: vision, briefings, feedback systems, MBWA.
learningObjectives: Explain management's role in internal communication.
Describe tools managers use to facilitate communication.
Recommend management actions for case firms.

simpleExplanation.title: Managers as communication enablers
simpleExplanation.summary: Managers must communicate vision clearly, run regular briefings, create channels for upward feedback, and stay visible on the shop floor — communication is an active leadership duty, not an optional extra.
steps: Hold regular team briefings and one-to-ones.
Use suggestion schemes and staff surveys.
Management by walking around (MBWA) — visible listening.

flashcards.back: Ensure messages flow up, down, and across — not just send emails.
Management by walking around — informal face-to-face on shop floor.
Align daily work with objectives; two-way Q&A.
Formal upward channel for ideas — rewards encourage use.
Anonymous pulse on morale and barriers — act on results.
Managers are primary communicators during restructure.
Surveys ignored — grapevine fills vacuum, trust falls.

sections.content: **Management facilitating communication** (7.2.5) closes **Topic 7.2**. Recommend **specific manager behaviours**, not vague 'communicate better'.

sections.question: Employee survey shows only 22% understand company strategy; 65% say 'management doesn't listen.' As HR director, recommend how line managers should improve communication.
sections.solution:
**Vision:** **Quarterly team briefings** with **consistent slides** from CEO — managers explain **local impact**.

**Upward feedback:** **Monthly one-to-ones** with documented actions; **digital suggestion box** with **published responses**.

**Visibility:** **MBWA** — ops managers spend **2 hours/week** on floor, not only in office.

**Accountability:** **Communication KPI** in manager appraisals — survey scores tracked **year on year**.

**Avoid:** One-off **town hall** without follow-up — worsens cynicism.
